children.heading: Actions Upon Receipt of Report.—
children.label: a Actions Upon Receipt of Report.—
A
children.text: may conduct any additional investigation which it considers to be necessary;

children.label: C
children.text: if the complaint is not dismissed, shall take such action as is appropriate to assure the effective and expeditious administration of the business of the courts within the circuit.

children.heading: Description of possible actions if complaint not dismissed.—
children.label: 2 Description of possible actions if complaint not dismissed.—
i
children.text: ordering that, on a temporary basis for a time certain, no further cases be assigned to the judge whose conduct is the subject of a complaint;

children.label: ii
children.text: censuring or reprimanding such judge by means of private communication; and

children.label: iii
children.text: censuring or reprimanding such judge by means of public announcement.

children.label: i
children.text: certifying disability of the judge pursuant to the procedures and standards provided under section 372(b); and

children.label: ii
children.text: requesting that the judge voluntarily retire, with the provision that the length of service requirements under shall not apply.section 371 of this title

children.heading: .—For magistrate judges
children.label: C .—For magistrate judges
children.text: If the conduct of a magistrate judge is the subject of the complaint, action by the judicial council under paragraph (1)(C) may include directing the chief judge of the district of the magistrate judge to take such action as the judicial council considers appropriate.

children.heading: Limitations on judicial council regarding removals.—
children.label: 3 Limitations on judicial council regarding removals.—

children.heading: .—Article iii judges
children.label: A .—Article iii judges
children.text: Under no circumstances may the judicial council order removal from office of any judge appointed to hold office during good behavior.

children.heading: .—Magistrate and bankruptcy judges
children.label: B .—Magistrate and bankruptcy judges
children.text: Any removal of a magistrate judge under this subsection shall be in accordance with section 631 and any removal of a bankruptcy judge shall be in accordance with section 152.

children.heading: .—Notice of action to judge
children.label: 4 .—Notice of action to judge
children.text: The judicial council shall immediately provide written notice to the complainant and to the judge whose conduct is the subject of the complaint of the action taken under this subsection.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: In addition to the authority granted under subsection (a), the judicial council may, in its discretion, refer any complaint under section 351, together with the record of any associated proceedings and its recommendations for appropriate action, to the Judicial Conference of the United States.

children.label: A
children.text: which might constitute one or more grounds for impeachment under article II of the Constitution, or

children.label: B
children.text: which, in the interest of justice, is not amenable to resolution by the judicial council,

children.heading: .—Notice to complainant and judge
children.label: 3 .—Notice to complainant and judge
children.text: A judicial council acting under authority of this subsection shall, unless contrary to the interests of justice, immediately submit written notice to the complainant and to the judge whose conduct is the subject of the action taken under this subsection.
